what_it_does: Node CLI linter that scans listed surface files and piece HTML files for literal hex colours and off-DNA easing words, classifies each finding, reports grouped samples per file, and exits nonzero when non-exempt findings remain.
observations: Loads dna/tokens.json and builds a colour value-to-address lookup from surface, accent, ink, semantic, scaffold, warm_pole, axis_ramp, and warmth_anchors entries.
Scans a fixed SURFACES list plus every .html file under immediate subdirectories of pieces.
Treats generated files and marked generated blocks as exempt locations for literal values.
Treats #000/#fff in mask-image or mask declarations as alpha stencil values rather than colour violations.
Marks archive/studio.html and tokens.html as archived and excludes their findings from violation counts.
Flags hand-written var fallbacks, known token literals, unknown colour literals, and selected bounce/spring/elastic/overshoot easing patterns.
Supports --all to include generated, mask, and archived hex findings in output.
Prints grouped finding counts with up to four raw@line samples and exits with status 0 only when total non-exempt findings are zero.
standouts: The file models lint output as classified findings rather than a single pass/fail list.
Registry-known literal colours are treated as addressability violations with suggested token names.
Generated output is explicitly treated as proof output and exempted by file path or block markers.
conventions: Uses ESM imports from node:fs, node:url, and node:path.
Keeps configuration data local as constants: SURFACES, ARCHIVED, GENERATED_FILES, GEN_BLOCK, HEX, and EASE_BAD.
Uses synchronous filesystem reads and directory walks suitable for a short-lived CLI script.
Stores findings as compact objects with k, lineNo, raw, and optional fix fields.
Groups report output by file and then by finding kind.
concerns: The fallback detector relies on the preceding 160 characters ending with a var(...) prefix, so formatting outside that window or comments between parts could affect classification.
Generated block detection uses a single indexOf per marker pair, so repeated generated blocks with the same markers would not all be represented.
Archived easing findings increment the exempt count but are not added to findings, even under --all.
The summary text says exempt masks + generated, while archived findings are also counted as exempt.
notes: A file-local proof linter that enforces colour address usage and motion vocabulary through classified source scans.
questions: Should --all include archived easing findings the same way it includes archived hex findings?
Can files contain multiple generated blocks with the same marker pair?
Is dropping alpha during hex normalization intended for all #rgba and #rrggbbaa matches?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it scans functioning surfaces and gallery pieces for raw colour values and off-DNA easings, classifies findings, exempts masks/generated/archived cases, reports violations, and exits by violation status.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the design-token registry operationally enforceable by detecting hand-written colour values and motion terms wherever design surfaces are authored.
novelty: The linter frames raw colour detection as an addressability proof, distinguishing generated literals, alpha masks, registry-token literals, foreign colours, and fallback drift in one report.
contribution: This file contributes a deterministic audit layer that maps registry colour values to token addresses, scans authored surfaces and pieces, classifies literal design substrates, and produces actionable per-file proof output for a fused ledger.
summary_for_embedding: Node ESM CLI lint script that builds a token colour address book, scans configured surface and piece files for literal hex colours and forbidden easing terms, classifies findings, reports grouped samples, and exits by violation count.
intention_for_embedding: It was for enforcing that authored design surfaces use registry addresses rather than copied colour values, while exempting generated output and mask stencils.

symbols.does: Adds a hex colour value and token name to the BOOK lookup when the hex value exists.
symbols.description: This helper uppercases the colour string and stores the associated address-like name in BOOK. It is used while translating token registry entries into a reverse lookup from literal colour value to token address.

symbols.does: Normalizes matched hex colour syntax to an uppercase six-digit RGB value.
symbols.description: This function uppercases input, expands #rgb to #rrggbb, expands #rgba while dropping alpha, and truncates #rrggbbaa to #rrggbb. The result is used for registry matching and mask colour checks.

symbols.does: Scans one file for hex colour literals and forbidden easing patterns, classifies findings, and updates global counts and per-file output data.
symbols.description: The function reads a file if it exists, computes generated ranges, checks each hex match against generated, mask, archived, fallback, token, and foreign classifications, then scans for off-DNA easing terms. It records findings with line numbers and optional fix text, increments violation or exempt counters, and appends files with visible findings to perFile.

symbols.does: Returns whether a source index falls inside any generated file or generated block range.
symbols.description: This file-local predicate closes over genRanges inside scan. It is used before other colour classifications so generated regions can be exempted from hand-authored literal checks.
